topic: You did not lose them, you lost who you imagined they would be
hook_seed: You did not lose them. You lost who you imagined
core_claim: Sometimes heartbreak is grief for the future you invented

topic: Moving on fast does not mean you did not care
hook_seed: Moving on fast does not mean you did not care
core_claim: It can mean you finally stopped abandoning yourself

topic: That feeling when a song hits different at 2am
hook_seed: If a song has ever ruined your night, this is for you
core_claim: Some memories return through your body before your logic catches up

topic: Talking every day does not make you close
hook_seed: Talking every day does not make you close
core_claim: Closeness is consistency, not constant access

topic: Four signs you are not over them that have nothing to do with texting
emotional_trigger: thinking you moved on but still reacting
hook_seed: Four signs you are not over them
core_claim: Your body can still be attached after your phone stops reaching

topic: What anxious attachment actually looks like in real life
hook_seed: Anxious attachment is not always loud
core_claim: Sometimes it looks like rereading one sentence until it hurts
It can mean you stopped performing hardness for unsafe people

topic: You keep explaining because part of you still wants permission
hook_seed: You keep explaining because you still want permission
core_claim: A boundary does not need to win a debate to be real

topic: If calm feels boring it might be because you are used to anxiety not love
emotional_trigger: feeling bored with a stable person
psych_concept: trauma bonding and nervous system dysregulation
hook_seed: If calm feels boring, this is for you
core_claim: You learned to read anxiety as excitement — calm reads as indifference

topic: You are not too much — you were just around people with too little
emotional_trigger: being told you are too intense or emotional
core_claim: Being called too much is often someone's limit, not your fault

topic: Why you miss them even when they were not even good for you
emotional_trigger: missing someone you know hurt you
hook_seed: Missing someone who hurt you is not weakness
core_claim: Intermittent reward creates stronger attachment than consistent care

topic: Silence in a relationship is not always neutral
emotional_trigger: feeling unseen without an argument to name it
hook_seed: Silence can be the loudest thing someone does to you
core_claim: Withdrawal without explanation is still a communication — it says you do not matter enough

topic: The difference between someone who is busy and someone who does not prioritize you
hook_seed: Busy people still find time for what matters to them
core_claim: Everyone has the same 24 hours — what differs is who they choose to spend them on

topic: You are not clingy — you just have not had consistent people
emotional_trigger: being called clingy or too needy
psych_concept: anxious attachment rooted in inconsistent caregiving
hook_seed: You are not clingy. You just have not had consistent people
core_claim: Needing reassurance is not weakness when the people around you have been inconsistent

topic: Some people leave because they were never ready to stay
emotional_trigger: questioning what you did wrong after someone left
hook_seed: Some people leave because they were never ready to stay
core_claim: Their leaving says more about their readiness than your worth
